reasoning: Anish Giri's March 2026 rating is 2753. A 1% increase would require him to have an Elo rating of at least 2781 on the Wikipedia FIDE Rankings page on April 5, 2026. The FIDE ratings on Wikipedia are updated monthly according to official FIDE lists. The April FIDE list will be published around April 1 and will only include games played leading up to April 1. The 2026 Candidates Tournament begins on March 28/29, which means those games will primarily conclude in April and will not be reflected on the April 1 list (FIDE typically only rates completed tournaments or delays them to the next month's list). Furthermore, elite chess players virtually never participate in classical tournaments in the final weeks before the Candidates as they dedicate that time to secret preparation and rest. Therefore, Giri is virtually guaranteed to not have played any rated classical games in March prior to the Candidates. Even if he had played a secret tournament, gaining 28 points at the 2750+ level in a single month requires an incredibly rare, historic performance (e.g., scoring +6 or +7 in a super-tournament), which would be major news. There is zero evidence he played such a tournament in March. Thus, his rating on the April 5 Wikipedia snapshot will almost certainly remain exactly 2753, making a 1% increase functionally impossible.
